sections.title: Market Pulse: Peace Hopes Drive Strong Gains, SpaceX Debuts

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Surge Amid Easing Geopolitical Tensions and SpaceX IPO
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed sharply higher today, rebounding from recent losses, as investor sentiment was bolstered by positive developments in Middle East geopolitical conflicts and the highly anticipated market debut of SpaceX. The broader market experienced a risk-on reversal, with lower oil prices and easing concerns contributing to a robust trading session. The AI trade also regained momentum, with key semiconductor stocks showing strong performance. Not financial advice.

items.headline: SpaceX Soars in Landmark IPO
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX made its highly anticipated market debut on Nasdaq, opening at $150 per share after a fixed offering price of $135, valuing the company at nearly $2 trillion. The debut created the world's first trillionaire, Elon Musk, and generated significant buzz in the aerospace and AI sectors.

items.headline: Micron Technology Rallies
items.summary: Shares of Micron Technology Inc. rallied significantly, benefiting from the broader rebound in AI-powered semiconductor stocks.

items.headline: Intel Jumps on AI Momentum
items.summary: Intel Corp. experienced a strong surge, recouping some losses amidst renewed investor confidence in the AI sector following positive geopolitical news.

items.headline: Advanced Micro Devices Up on Strong Outlook
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. saw an 8% increase in its stock price. Analysts at Citi maintain a buy rating with a $575 price target, anticipating earnings exceeding $20 per share by 2028, largely driven by expected significant contributions from Meta's AI products.

items.headline: Adobe Shares Slip on CFO Departure News
items.summary: Adobe shares were under pressure despite reporting better-than-expected Q2 earnings with record revenue of $6.62 billion, driven by AI demand. The stock dropped following news of its CFO's exit, leading to multiple analyst downgrades from Stifel, Wolfe Research, and Evercore ISI.

items.headline: Crude Oil Plunges on Peace Hopes
items.summary: WTI crude oil prices settled at $85.86 per barrel, plunging 5.4% from its daily high. This significant drop was primarily driven by easing U.S.-Iran tensions and hopes for a peace deal that could lift oil sanctions and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: May PPI Rises Sharply, Core PPI Slows
items.summary: The U.S. Producer Price Index (PPI) for May rose 6.5% year-on-year, marking the largest increase since November 2022, primarily due to surging energy prices and transportation costs. However, core PPI, excluding volatile food and energy, unexpectedly slowed to 4.9% year-on-year, suggesting a widening gap with CPI that could squeeze corporate profit margins. Month-over-month, headline PPI increased 1.1%, surpassing estimates.

items.headline: ECB Raises Rates for First Time Since 2023
items.summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) raised its deposit rate to 2.250%, main refinancing rate to 2.400%, and marginal lending rate to 2.650%. This marks the first rate hike since 2023, aligning with market expectations and reflecting efforts to counter inflationary pressures exacerbated by geopolitical events.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Ease, Peace Deal Imminent
items.summary: Market sentiment improved significantly on hopes of a nearing U.S.-Iran peace deal. President Trump announced the cancellation of planned strikes and stated that an agreement is largely finalized, potentially to be signed in Europe this weekend. This de-escalation is expected to lift oil sanctions and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Mixed Sector Performance, AI Semiconductors Rebound
items.summary: On the sector front, four out of eleven sectors of the S&P 500 finished in positive territory, while seven ended in negative territory. The Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR ($XLP) rose 1.7% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) gained 1.5%. Conversely, the Information Technology Select Sector SPDR ($XLK), Industrials Select Sector SPDR ($XLI), and Materials Select Sector SPDR ($XLB) fell 2.3%, 3.4%, and 2.3% respectively. AI-powered semiconductor stocks, however, staged a strong rebound after recent losses.

items.headline: Roots Corp. Reports Q1 Loss Despite Sales Increase
items.summary: Roots Corp. announced a Q1 loss of $10.1 million, or 26 cents per share, compared to a $7.9 million loss a year prior. However, sales for the quarter increased to $42.6 million from $40.0 million year-over-year, driven by growth in both direct-to-consumer and partner channels. The company continues its strategic review, including a possible sale.

items.headline: Children's Place (PLCE) Expected to Report Earnings
items.summary: Children's Place ($PLCE) is among the major companies expected to report their earnings today, June 12, 2026. Investors are closely watching options activity for insights into potential post-earnings stock movements.
